title: Reports: Sixers to acquire Jaylen Brown from Celtics
summary: NBA.com cites ESPN, The Athletic and The Boston Globe reporting that Philadelphia will acquire Jaylen Brown for Paul George and four draft picks.

title: Reports: Mitchell Robinson joining Celtics on 3-year deal
summary: NBA.com cites ESPN reporting that former Knicks center Mitchell Robinson has agreed to a three-year, $47.4 million deal with Boston.

title: Report: Spurs agree to 2-year deal with Tobias Harris
summary: NBA.com cites ESPN's Shams Charania reporting that Tobias Harris has agreed to a two-year, $31 million deal with San Antonio.

title: Report: Pacers agree to 2-year deal with Kelly Oubre Jr.
summary: NBA.com cites ESPN's Shams Charania reporting that Kelly Oubre Jr. has agreed to a two-year deal worth nearly $17 million with Indiana.

title: Reports: De'Anthony Melton agrees to return to Warriors
summary: NBA.com cites multiple reports, including ESPN, that De'Anthony Melton has agreed to a two-year, $11 million deal to stay with Golden State.

title: Reports: Lakers agree to deals with Mamukelashvili, Sexton and Grimes
summary: NBA.com cites multiple reports that the Lakers have reached agreements with Sandro Mamukelashvili, Collin Sexton and Quentin Grimes.

title: Reports: Bulls, Norman Powell agree to 2-year deal
summary: NBA.com cites ESPN and Chicago Sports Network reporting that All-Star guard Norman Powell has agreed to a two-year, $45 million deal with Chicago.

title: Reports: Lakers add Walker Kessler in trade with Jazz
summary: NBA.com cites multiple reports that the Lakers will acquire Walker Kessler from Utah for two first-round picks and two pick swaps.

title: John Collins, Pistons reportedly agree to 3-year deal
summary: NBA.com cites ESPN's Shams Charania reporting that Detroit has agreed to a three-year, $51 million deal with John Collins.

title: 2026 Free Agency: Options and qualifying offers
summary: NBA.com's official roundup tracks player options, team options and qualifying offers as the 2026 free-agency period opens.

title: Reports: LeBron James will play for team other than Lakers
summary: NBA.com cites multiple reports that Rich Paul told outlets LeBron James plans to leave the Lakers and continue his career elsewhere in 2026-27.
